steps.title: Factor the difference of squares
steps.content:
Using the identity $a^2 - b^2 = (a-b)(a+b)$, rewrite the first equation:
$$x^2 - y^2 = (x - y)(x + y)$$
Since the left side equals 39, we have
$$(x - y)(x + y) = 39.$$

steps.title: Substitute the known difference
steps.content:
We are given $x - y = 3$. Substitute into the factored form:
$$3 \cdot (x + y) = 39$$
Divide both sides by 3:
$$x + y = \frac{39}{3} = 13.$$
That matches choice C — C is correct.
